{"id":"project-LICHEN-rnev","title":"TofuPeerResolver.pin_peer may raise during EDHOC","description":"File: python/src/lichen/coap/secure.py:200-208

pin_peer raises ValueError if a peer's key changes:
```python
async def pin_peer(self, host: str, pubkey: bytes) -> None:
    async with self._lock:
        if host in self._pinned:
            if self._pinned[host] != pubkey:
                raise ValueError(
                    f'TOFU violation: peer {host} key changed '
                    f'(possible MITM or hardware swap)'
                )
```

This is called from SecureDatagramChannel._establish_context (line 482-483) after OSCORE context is already stored. If the peer legitimately replaced their hardware and has a new key, the exception:
1. Leaves an invalid OSCORE context in the store
2. Does not clean up _pending_edhoc
3. Causes all future communications with the peer to fail

Fix: Check for key mismatch before storing the context, or provide a mechanism to reset/replace pinned keys.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"me@mark.atwood.name","created_at":"2026-07-12T06:18:26.334899Z","created_by":"Mark Atwood","updated_at":"2026-07-12T06:18:26.334933Z"}
